---
title: "Extreme Value Analysis of Potomac River Discharge"
author: "Francis Yap"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  cache = FALSE
)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(knitr)
  library(scales)
  library(htmltools)
})

dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Source the computational engine.
# Keep this R script in the same folder as this Rmd.
source("potomac_results_submission_final.r")

# Helper for embedding saved PDF outputs in HTML.
embed_pdf <- function(path, height = "520px") {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    return(HTML(paste0(
      "<p><em>File not found: ", path, "</em></p>"
    )))
  }

  tags$iframe(
    src = path,
    width = "100%",
    height = height,
    style = "border: 1px solid #ddd;"
  )
}

fmt_cfs <- function(x) {
  scales::comma(round(x, 0))
}
```

```{r convert-pdfs, include=FALSE}
if (!requireNamespace("pdftools", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("pdftools")
}

pdf_files <- list.files(
  fig_dir,
  pattern = "\\.pdf$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

png_files <- sub("\\.pdf$", ".png", pdf_files)

for (i in seq_along(pdf_files)) {
  if (!file.exists(png_files[i])) {
    pdftools::pdf_convert(
      pdf = pdf_files[i],
      format = "png",
      dpi = 300,
      pages = 1,
      filenames = png_files[i]
    )
  }
}
```
# Executive Summary

This project applies extreme-value methods to annual maximum daily discharge
from the Potomac River at Little Falls. The analysis estimates return levels
using Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) and Gumbel models, evaluates uncertainty
with profile-likelihood confidence intervals, and studies sampling behavior
through parametric and year-resampling simulations.

The workflow is separated into two layers:

The R script contains the computational engine.
This R Markdown report loads cached results and presents the analysis.

Heavy simulations are cached as .rds files so that the report can be
re-rendered quickly after the first complete run.

## Key Findings

- GEV model provides better tail fit than Gumbel  
- Uncertainty increases substantially for return periods beyond 100 years  
- Simulation shows bias decreases as record length increases  
- Confidence interval coverage improves with sample size but remains imperfect for small samples

# Reproducibility Settings
```{r}
tibble::tibble(
  setting = c(
    "Random seed",
    "Return periods for empirical analysis",
    "Simulation record lengths",
    "Simulation return periods",
    "Parametric simulation replicates",
    "Year-resampling replicates",
    "Figure output directory"
  ),
  value = c(
    "8010",
    paste(T_vals_res, collapse = ", "),
    paste(n_grid_sim, collapse = ", "),
    paste(periods_sim, collapse = ", "),
    as.character(B_param),
    as.character(B_resamp),
    "figures/"
  )
) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Main reproducibility settings used in the analysis.")
```
Load or Run Analysis Objects

The following chunks load cached .rds files when available. If the cache files
do not exist, the corresponding workflow is executed and saved for future use.
```{r}
results_file <- "outputs/results_obj.rds"

if (file.exists(results_file)) {
  results_obj <- readRDS(results_file)
} else {
  results_obj <- run_results_workflow()
  saveRDS(results_obj, results_file)
}
```

```{r}
param_file <- "outputs/sim_param_obj.rds"

if (file.exists(param_file)) {
  sim_param_obj <- readRDS(param_file)
} else {
  sim_param_obj <- run_parametric_simulation(
    mu0 = results_obj$gev_ismev_mle["mu"],
    sigma0 = results_obj$gev_ismev_mle["sigma"],
    xi0 = results_obj$gev_ismev_mle["xi"]
  )

  saveRDS(sim_param_obj, param_file)
}
```

```{r}
resamp_file <- "outputs/sim_resamp_obj.rds"

if (file.exists(resamp_file)) {
  sim_resamp_obj <- readRDS(resamp_file)
} else {
  sim_resamp_obj <- run_year_resampling_simulation(
    daily_df = results_obj$daily,
    mu0 = results_obj$gev_ismev_mle["mu"],
    sigma0 = results_obj$gev_ismev_mle["sigma"],
    xi0 = results_obj$gev_ismev_mle["xi"]
  )

  saveRDS(sim_resamp_obj, resamp_file)
}
```

```{r}
run_simulation_supplement(sim_param_obj, sim_resamp_obj)
```
# Data Overview
```{r}
data_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  quantity = c(
    "Daily observations",
    "Annual maxima",
    "First year",
    "Last year",
    "Minimum annual maximum discharge",
    "Median annual maximum discharge",
    "Maximum annual maximum discharge"
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(results_obj$daily),
    nrow(results_obj$am),
    min(results_obj$am$year),
    max(results_obj$am$year),
    fmt_cfs(min(results_obj$am$block_max)),
    fmt_cfs(median(results_obj$am$block_max)),
    fmt_cfs(max(results_obj$am$block_max))
  )
)

knitr::kable(data_summary, caption = "Summary of the daily series and annual maxima.")
```
# Empirical Extreme-Value Analysis

## Annual Maxima and Stationarity

The annual maximum series is the basis for the block-maxima extreme-value
analysis. A linear smoother is shown as a visual diagnostic, while formal
trend evidence is summarized separately using the Mann-Kendall test and a
stationary versus nonstationary GEV likelihood-ratio comparison.
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_stationarity.png"))
```

```{r}
mk_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "project_mann_kendall_summary.csv")
lrt_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "project_stationarity_lrt_summary.csv")

if (file.exists(mk_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(mk_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    knitr::kable(digits = 4, caption = "Mann-Kendall trend-test summary.")
}

if (file.exists(lrt_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(lrt_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    knitr::kable(digits = 4, caption = "Stationary versus nonstationary GEV likelihood-ratio summary.")
}
```
## Model Diagnostic Plot

The Q-Q plot compares empirical annual maxima against fitted model quantiles.
Closer agreement with the identity line indicates better distributional fit.
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_qq.png"))
```
## Return-Level Estimates

Return levels are estimated for both GEV and Gumbel models using
profile-likelihood confidence intervals. Uncertainty increases for longer
return periods because these estimates require greater extrapolation into
the upper tail.
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_return_levels_ci.png"))
```

```{r}
results_obj$rl_ci_long |>
  filter(T %in% c(10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 200)) |>
  mutate(
    RL = fmt_cfs(RL),
    CI_low = fmt_cfs(CI_low),
    CI_high = fmt_cfs(CI_high)
  ) |>
  arrange(model, T) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "Selected return-level estimates with 95% confidence intervals."
  )
```
## Confidence-Interval Widths

The width of the return-level confidence interval provides a direct summary
of estimation uncertainty. Wider intervals at larger return periods reflect
the difficulty of estimating rare events from a finite annual-maxima record.
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_return_levels_ciwidth.png"))
```

```{r}
xi_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "project_xi_profile_ci.csv")

if (file.exists(xi_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(xi_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    mutate(
      Estimate = round(Estimate, 4),
      CI_low = round(CI_low, 4),
      CI_high = round(CI_high, 4)
    ) |>
    knitr::kable(caption = "Profile-likelihood confidence interval for the GEV shape parameter.")
}
```
## Supplementary Empirical Diagnostics

Daily Series and Annual Maxima
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_daily_series_with_annual_maxima.png"))
```
Seasonality of Monthly Maxima
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_monthly_maxima_seasonality.png"))
```
Month of Annual Maximum Occurrence
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "project_annual_maxima_month_counts.png"))
```

```{r}
month_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "project_annual_maxima_month_counts.csv")

if (file.exists(month_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(month_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    knitr::kable(caption = "Month-of-occurrence counts for annual maximum discharge.")
}
```
## Parametric Simulation Study

The parametric simulation evaluates how well the GEV estimation workflow
recovers known return levels when samples are generated directly from the
fitted GEV model. This provides a controlled assessment of bias, RMSE,
coverage, and confidence-interval width as record length increases.

Parameter Estimation
```{r}
sim_param_obj$param_sum |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Parametric simulation summary for GEV parameter estimates.")
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_xi_boxplot.png"))
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_param_bias_vs_n.png"))
```
Return-Level Bias and RMSE
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_rl_bias_vs_n_mean.png"))
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_rl_rmse_vs_n.png"))
```
Confidence-Interval Coverage

Coverage is reported conditional on successful confidence-interval fits.
This distinction matters because profile-likelihood intervals may occasionally
fail for short or unstable simulated samples.
```{r}
sim_param_obj$ci_sum |>
  mutate(
    coverage = round(coverage, 3),
    mean_width = fmt_cfs(mean_width)
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Parametric simulation confidence-interval coverage and mean width.")
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_coverage_vs_n.png"))
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_ci_width_vs_n.png"))
```
GEV versus Gumbel Simulation Comparison
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_gumbel_vs_gev_bias_mean.png"))
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_gumbel_vs_gev_rmse_mean.png"))
```
Distribution of Estimated 100-Year Return Levels
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "sim_param_rl_distribution_100yr.png"))
```

```{r}
param_rl100_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "sim_param_rl100_summary_stats.csv")

if (file.exists(param_rl100_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(param_rl100_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2))) |>
    knitr::kable(caption = "Parametric simulation summary statistics for the estimated 100-year return level.")
}
```
## Year-Resampling Robustness Study

The year-resampling experiment resamples full years from the observed daily
record, recomputes annual maxima, and refits the GEV model. This assesses
how sensitive return-level estimates are to the available historical record.

Reference Inclusion and Interval Width
```{r}
sim_resamp_obj$ci |>
  mutate(
    coverage_reference = round(coverage_reference, 3),
    mean_width = fmt_cfs(mean_width)
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Year-resampling reference inclusion rates and mean confidence-interval widths.")
```

```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "resample_coverage_vs_n.png"))
```
Distribution of Estimated 100-Year Return Levels
```{r, out.width="95%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "resample_rl_distribution_100yr.png"))
```

```{r}
resamp_rl100_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "sim_resample_rl100_summary_stats.csv")

if (file.exists(resamp_rl100_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(resamp_rl100_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2))) |>
    knitr::kable(caption = "Year-resampling summary statistics for the estimated 100-year return level.")
}
```
## Generated Output Files

The following files are the final figures, tables, diagnostics, and cached
objects used in this report. Earlier exploratory outputs have been excluded
to keep the results concise and reproducible.
```{r generated-files}
final_output_files <- c(
  "project_stationarity.png",
  "project_mann_kendall_summary.csv",
  "project_stationarity_lrt_summary.csv",
  "project_qq.png",
  "project_return_levels_ci.png",
  "project_return_levels_ci_diagnostics.csv",
  "project_return_levels_ciwidth.png",
  "project_xi_profile_ci.csv",
  "project_daily_series_with_annual_maxima.png",
  "project_monthly_maxima_seasonality.png",
  "project_annual_maxima_month_counts.png",
  "project_annual_maxima_month_counts.csv",
  "sim_xi_boxplot.png",
  "sim_param_bias_vs_n.png",
  "sim_rl_bias_vs_n_mean.png",
  "sim_rl_rmse_vs_n.png",
  "sim_ci_summary.csv",
  "sim_coverage_vs_n.png",
  "sim_ci_width_vs_n.png",
  "sim_gumbel_vs_gev_bias_mean.png",
  "sim_gumbel_vs_gev_rmse_mean.png",
  "sim_param_rl_distribution_100yr.png",
  "sim_param_rl100_summary_stats.csv",
  "resample_ci_summary.csv",
  "resample_coverage_vs_n.png",
  "resample_rl_distribution_100yr.png",
  "sim_resample_rl100_summary_stats.csv"
)

generated_files <- tibble::tibble(
  file = c(
    final_output_files,
    "results_obj.rds",
    "sim_param_obj.rds",
    "sim_resamp_obj.rds"
  ),
  location = c(
    rep("figures/", length(final_output_files)),
    rep("outputs/", 3)
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    exists = c(
      file.exists(file.path(fig_dir, final_output_files)),
      file.exists(file.path("outputs", "results_obj.rds")),
      file.exists(file.path("outputs", "sim_param_obj.rds")),
      file.exists(file.path("outputs", "sim_resamp_obj.rds"))
    )
  )

generated_files |>
  dplyr::filter(exists) |>
  dplyr::select(file, location) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Final analysis outputs used in this report.")
```
All listed files are reproducible from the provided R script and cached objects.

## Reproducibility Information
```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
